title: 59. Encode and Decode Strings

examples.explanation: The encoded string is decoded back to the original list.
A list containing an empty string is preserved.
An empty list remains empty after encoding and decoding.

hints: Think of a way to delimit each string so that decoding is unambiguous.
A common approach is to prefix each string with its length followed by a delimiter like '#'.
During decoding, read the length until the delimiter, then read that many characters as the next string.
